transcript.text: But I would just be very intentional about how to structure the campaigns.
And I'll show you even what we're doing.
mean, we don't, we're ramping up our own campaigns.
mean, we spend like around three grand a month on LinkedIn ads, which isn't a ton, obviously, just because we source a lot from organic.
But, you know, the campaigns that work really well and print a lot of meetings are the ones that are very targeted.
So basically what we did was I set up a campaign that's specific to cybersecurity.
And I created a bunch of content organically on LinkedIn about how we help cybersecurity companies optimize their ad campaigns.
So there's one specific ad here.
So it's like we just audited three cybersecurity companies.
So it's a very, like, obviously targeted ad.
And then after it was running organically for a little bit, it just got...
Decent amount of impressions and comments.
I went in and added a call to action where people can actually book a time to talk about their ad campaigns.
And we've actually closed a decent amount of revenue from this.
closed a publicly traded company from this ad, which is pretty crazy.
Actually, I remember taking that call in the GrowthX office, which is funny, you know?
that was the one?
Yeah, it came from this.
But we had a call on Monday from this campaign as well.
So just to give you the idea, it's like, you know, we're calling out the exact persona who we're going after.
If we look at the actual structure of the campaign itself, like, you know, it's all these marketing titles that anybody in the computer network and data security software products industry.
So there's like a very tight alignment.
I think you guys could probably do a ton of similar stuff.
Like, we just revamped this financial or fintech company.
mean, they're...
they're...
blog or something like that.
This is all the stuff we did to do that.
If you're interested in learning more, schedule time here and then do the exact same kind of play.
But creating content around the industries that you're working with works really well in LinkedIn.
My industry is interesting.
Yeah.
Well, I guess that seems quite straightforward.
then like, what is the conversion?
Do you like conversion track?

Is there?
Yeah, you can actually track UTMs through Calendly.

But yeah, we can track all of the bookings.
That's how I know these leads.
Oh, so that link goes directly to Calendly.
Yeah, exactly.
That link goes directly to Calendly with UTMs appended.
So we can see those leads came from that ad specifically.
But there's also a few other things you can do.
So something we set up all the time for our clients.
Just skip that.
And were those ads, like, or sorry, were those leads that came in a little bit noisier than, like, the typical, I don't know if you guys mostly source, like, inbound or, like, from socials?
So, like, one of our problems is, like, we only have one and a half salespeople, and it's, like, I don't know if I can flood his calendar.
I find the leads coming from that campaign are very qualified, and they're not, like, tire kickers or anything like that, so.
Okay.
Yeah, I think it just depends on the campaign you set up, but what I was going to say is for conversions as well, you can set up lead forms in LinkedIn.

don't know if you've played around with these at all, but they convert really well, and just a trick is you can switch email address for a work email, and then, essentially, you validate it, so no, people can't submit Yahoo or Gmail or personal email, which is nice.
And then another field we always ask for.
Or it is LinkedIn profile URL, which is great.
And these are pre-populated fields.
So all the person has to do is, you know, open the form and submit their information.
And it immediately gets sent to the ad account.
You know, you could edit the copy, like book a demo or whatever offer you want to actually advertise.
then edit it.
And then what happens is there's a confirmation message.
So this is another place where you can redirect them to a calendar link.
And so they submit their information and then they can actually book the time for visiting that link.
Cool.
I guess you could do this, like, for a lead magnet to, like, you can use this for anything, really.
Yeah, exactly.
You could even use it for signups for whatever, like, top of funnel, like, content thing you're going to, you want to do.
And then you can, these LinkedIn lead forms are really easy to integrate with other platforms, like HubSpot.
Directly integrates them with them.
So
You can pull in all the leads that are coming through in real time, or you could use Zapier if you need, so.
Okay, that's great.
Okay, so that one's super straightforward.
Is the definition of the audience, like, usually that simple, or, like, I know you can upload people, or you can define it based on, like, signals from the pixel.
Like, do you guys do any of that?
Yeah, definitely.
So, there's a bunch of different types of audiences.
You can do company or contact list.
So, what you can do is upload a list of companies and then overlay the job titles on top of the companies that you want to go after, which is pretty predictable.
I mean, you have the exact person at the company who has the current title.
Or you could do a contact list upload.
So, if you have a list of email addresses, you can upload that.
It's trickier to get a good match rate for contact lists because most people use their personal email for LinkedIn.
But they're getting better.
So, So, So, So, So, So,
I'm seeing higher and higher match rates for contact lists, and I would say it's better for enterprise targeting.
Because, you know, at one enterprise company, there could be a billion different contacts that you might end up hitting in your targeting.
But if you have the exact person at the large company that you want to reach, it's a good way to do it.
But company lists are pretty reliable, too.
Is there, like, life cycle of an ad?
Like, example, like, when I see company, like, an interesting thing would be, like, I mean, we get signals from a lot of places.
So it's, like, cool.

If we took the list of companies that, like, have ever come to an event, liked our LinkedIn posts, like, maybe de-anonymized on our website, and just, like, threw that into a list, and I'm always going to target the, like, VP of marketing there.
Is that something that I would just, like, keep on forever, maybe?
Or maybe that's, like, too not targeted to do?
Because it's not by industry, I guess.
Like, there any form where it's like people kind of trickle into this list almost because they're engaging or sending a signal somewhere else?
Yeah, for sure.
So like I mentioned, HubSpot integrates with LinkedIn ads, so you could set up a dynamic audience.
I forget, do you guys use HubSpot?
We use HubSpot.
Yeah, let's say you just have a dynamic list in HubSpot.
Whenever you update that, it would also update in LinkedIn.
So if you have a campaign that's targeting certain job titles on a dynamic list, yeah, would be doing exactly what you described.
We actually are using Clay's beta integration, so we're kind of doing the same thing through Clay as well, pushing like dynamic lists.
But yeah, it's totally possible.
Cool.
Yeah.
Seems like this could get sophisticated, I guess.
And then is there anything you'd like, because I guess the phase I'm at is about like, well, what's this?
Validated, I will come to you and ask you to help me run it.
So I'm just trying to at least do the zero to one before I try to delegate or scale it up.
Is there anything you'd recommend to answer this question of like, is there any value out of doing ads on LinkedIn?
How would you structure that experiment?
I mean, there's definitely value.
I mean, we're, I feel like in a lot of ways we target the same people.
So yeah, so the fact that we're seeing value out of it, I think it's a no brainer.
Just the fact that like sales and marketing people are all over LinkedIn.
I would just define the goals very concretely when you launch the test.
And I would try not to launch like too many different offerings.
That's why I think you guys should prioritize probably like one of the service lines or or however you want to think about it.
Just.
Don't spread the budget too thin across the different ads that you're running, but also the different products that you're advertising.
So I would just go in with a very tight experiment plan.
Okay, so if you want to generate a meeting, or if your goal is to generate meetings, set up the campaigns in a way that you are offering something, you're advertising a demo, something like that.
If it's more of an awareness thing, track the companies that are engaging with the ads, that kind of thing.
I don't know if you guys, I mean, do you take like more of an account-based approach to your, like, what do you think about it?

It's really not, like, we have like almost no motion, it's all just like completely, like, inbound because of all this content we put out, and they just kind of self-select so the right person reaches out.
That happens a lot.
Mm-hmm.
Mm Mm Mm-hmm.
Plus, like, our own network or investors refer them.
Like, we almost never, like, go out to, like, target somebody.
So, like, when we do sell, like, it is, I mean, same, maybe same as what you do.
Like, it gets complicated because of how the price, I guess.
Yeah.
So there usually is more than one person that cares.
So, like, if we did go out, yeah, it would be account-based for sure.
Yeah.
So I would get a list uploaded.
I would also make sure that you have all the tracking in place as well.
Like, installing the insight tag is what LinkedIn calls their pixel.
And set up conversion tracking so you just understand, like, how the people that are coming in who are actually engaging with your website in some meaningful way.
Like, because if you don't have tracking, it's going to be really hard to measure the success of it.
Thank you.
Thank
But it seems like with the form, kind of, like the conversion just happens there and then it goes to Calendly, right?
Yeah, yeah, for sure.
So that'll be the thing you want to identify too.
Like, are you just going to run the lead form campaigns?
Like, is that your goal is to drive as many demos as possible?
Yeah, I mean, I think I'll probably just try that.
mean, it seems like, yeah, like what you showed is really interesting.
Probably just try that.
it's like campaign for, yeah, like campaign per goal.
So like, maybe I would have one that's like direct to account lead, another one that might be to a lead magnet, like a workshop recording maybe or something like that.
And then just based on who gets it, like, I guess we'll follow up with emails, but you can eyeball, like, cool, is that list qualified?
Does that actually work?
Probably do that.
Any suggestions?
Yeah, I would say for you guys, would probably want to allocate a little bit more.
I mean, like we're in the proof of concept stage and we're already starting to scale more and more with the spend.
So I would aim between what we're spending to probably on the higher end, like five to 10K a month in total spend.
So for you, like scaling spend would mean what?
Like adding more personas or literally just dialing up the dollars that LinkedIn can spend?
I wouldn't add personas just for the sake of it.
I think for you guys, it would be more coverage, like making sure you can reach all of the accounts that you want to go after at the core level.
Well, I think that's a...
I think that's it.
I will report back and let you know if this works.
Cool.
Can I ask how much it was, like, qualified meeting, like, dollars-wise for that campaign you're looking at?
Yeah, so it's around, I think, like, $300, $350.
Okay.
Yeah.
Something we also do a lot with our clients are message ads.
We don't do a ton of them internally, but you can set up the, like, LinkedIn message ads.
Wait, what's a message ad?
Is that the same thing as a mail, or do they just change the name of it?
It's different.
So let me show you.
Here's an example from a client.
So it, I don't know, I'm sure you've seen this, but they come through and say sponsored, and it's essentially very...
Very similar to an InMail, and then you are given like a call to action, and then when they click this, that lead form that I was just showing you pops up.
That's so interesting.
I feel like I can't get it.
I feel like I've seen it, but now when I look, it doesn't look like there's any in my inbox.
Yeah, here, I can actually send myself a test message, and I can show you exactly what the flow is.
That could work really well, and is the targeting for that different, or is it the same thing, like you're targeting by Persona?
Same thing as what I was showing you before, and I think it would work really well coming from Marcel.
Yeah.
So you can see here it says sponsored.
You can see the first 50 characters, very similar to the other messages.
That pops up, and then you can hit request demo, and then that form that I was just showing you also.
Presumably you can do, okay, cool.
These work really well.
Like the, you can set up a funnel basically when you're sponsoring the, anybody who engages with that, you can set up a retargeting segment and then have messages coming from the same profile.
So they engage with the ad comments or like on it, like it, and then they get one of these message ads afterwards.
So the tunnel, the funnel is really tight.
Yeah, yeah, that's what I was thinking.
Okay, that's great.
Thank you so much.
Of course, sure.
Anything else you wanted to talk about?
No, not really.
I think it's pretty clear.
Run this experiment, see if it does anything.
I feel like it definitely should.
I think the main thing, it's like, the hard part probably is the...
Getting the call, it's like, can we design the system so that we don't need a million salespeople to, like, take the calls?
Because we're kind of already bottlenecked.
think our main salesperson is, like, 20-something open deals, and he barely has enough time for that.
And to take more prospect calls and, like, nurture them along the early phases.
So I'm not really sure what to do with that.
Maybe we just need more salespeople.
I think we're hiring more.
Okay.
Yeah, I mean, sounds like it.
That's a good place to be in, for sure.
But maybe there's...
That's why it's like, I almost want to, like, protect it a little bit.
But maybe let's, like, worry about that after I drive too many deals, you know?
Yeah, for sure.
I mean, I think it's definitely worth investing now while you have that, I guess, breathing room.
Yeah, exactly.
And that segment is definitely something you could be advertising to with case studies, like, open opportunities.
Like, I think that's definitely something you could push from HubSpot.
Thank you.
Thank
Do LinkedIn ads, and add that to a retargeting segment.
Oh, wait, wait, wait, sorry, so make a segment of open opportunities, and then show them case studies, is that what you're saying?

Yeah, like, Marcel has a great post about working with a certain company, and then you sponsor that post to that audience to make sure every single person, that company, knows that you guys have worked with that company in past.
do that as well, like, I don't know if you've been on our website lately, but if you go on our website, I'm sure you'll get some retargeting ads.
We record a bunch of nice testimonials from our clients, and we're basically just running those retargeting campaigns on all channels.
Right.
Oh, maybe last question, I think you told me last time, but, like, what's the model for working with you guys on ads?
What's the, um, like, the commercials?
Uh, no, no, just, like, uh...
How do I, like, if we were to work with you guys on ads as well to help, like, get help there, like, what does that look like?
Yeah, for sure.
So very similar to what we're doing for you for GoToMarketEngineering, you'd be staffed with a channel expert.

So you could either work with us on just paid social, so just LinkedIn ads, or you could do paid social and paid search, and you would have a channel specialist dedicated to both channels, or, like, one per channel.
And then, obviously, you would have me for oversight on everything, and Chris, our head of paid search, if you want to run Google or YouTube ads in the future.
Okay.
And is it, like, spend-based?
Like, how do you guys...
It's tiers of spend.
So anything from up to $50,000 a month in spend across all channels.
On one channel is $8,000, for two, it's $12,000.
Got it.
So that's, like, I'll spend, like, if I spend $50,000, anything other...
Yeah, exactly.
And then it's 50 to 150, and then 150 to 300.
We can talk about that when we get there.
Okay.
Sounds good.
And then term.
I think with stuff Alex is doing, it's like six months?
Yeah, it's the exact same term.
Okay.
Okay.
All right, man.
Thank you so much.
Of course.
If you have any other questions, just let me know.
Happy to help.
Okay.
Sounds good.
All right, man.
See you, Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Ali advises Jason on a LinkedIn ad strategy for GrowthX.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Strategy:** Use hyper-targeted campaigns (e.g., ad + content for one industry) to generate high-quality leads, as Understory's internal campaigns prove the model works.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=4.0)
  - [**Tactics:** Use LinkedIn Lead Forms for high conversion and UTMs on direct Calendly links for precise tracking.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=144.0)
  - [**Experiment:** Start with a tight experiment (one service, one goal) and a $5k–$10k/mo budget to validate the channel's value before scaling.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=473.0)
  - [**Retargeting:** Use dynamic HubSpot lists to retarget warm audiences (e.g., open opportunities) with relevant content like case studies.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=1010.0)

## Topics

### The Understory Playbook: Hyper-Targeted Campaigns

  - [Understory's internal campaigns (\~$3k/mo spend) generate qualified meetings by targeting specific personas within a single industry.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=4.0)
  - [**Example:** A cybersecurity campaign (ad + organic content) generated a call from a publicly traded company.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=41.0)
  - [**Cost per Qualified Meeting:** \~$300–$350.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=784.0)

### Core Tactics: Lead Capture & Tracking

  - [**Direct-to-Calendly:**](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=144.0)
      - [**Process:** Ad → Calendly link with UTMs.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=144.0)
      - [**Why:** UTMs enable precise tracking of booking source.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=144.0)
  - [**LinkedIn Lead Forms:**](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=210.0)
      - [**Process:** Ad → Pre-populated form → Confirmation message with Calendly link.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=210.0)
      - [**Why:** High conversion rates; can validate for work emails only.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=210.0)
      - [**Integration:** Connects directly to HubSpot or via Zapier.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=291.0)

### Audience Targeting: Precision & Automation

  - [**Company List Uploads:**](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=313.0)
      - [**Process:** Upload a list of target companies → overlay job titles.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=313.0)
      - [**Why:** Highly predictable and reliable targeting.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=313.0)
  - [**Dynamic HubSpot Lists:**](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=429.0)
      - [**Process:** Connect HubSpot to LinkedIn Ads.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=429.0)
      - [**Why:** Automatically syncs audiences, enabling retargeting of warm leads (e.g., website visitors, event attendees) with specific content.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=429.0)

### Experiment Plan for GrowthX

  - [**Goal:** Validate if LinkedIn ads can drive value for GrowthX.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=473.0)
  - [**Structure:**](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=523.0)
      - [**Focus:** One service line, one goal (e.g., demo bookings).](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=523.0)
      - [**Budget:** $5k–$10k/mo.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=727.0)
      - [**Tracking:** Install the LinkedIn Insight Tag (pixel) and set up conversion tracking.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=635.0)
  - [**Potential Bottleneck:** GrowthX's single salesperson is already at capacity with 20+ open deals.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=955.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Validate the channel first; the bottleneck is a good problem to solve.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=955.0)

### Advanced Tactic: Message Ads

  - [**Function:** Sponsored messages appearing in the LinkedIn inbox.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=800.0)
  - [**Flow:** Message with CTA → LinkedIn Lead Form.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=833.0)
  - [**Why:** Effective for retargeting users who have already engaged with a standard ad.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=907.0)

### Understory Agency Services

  - [**Model:** Staffing with a dedicated channel expert and senior oversight.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=1087.0)
  - [**Pricing (6-month term):**](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=1117.0)
      - [**$8k/mo:** One channel (e.g., Paid Social)](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=1117.0)
      - [**$12k/mo:** Two channels (e.g., Paid Social + Paid Search)](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=1117.0)
      - [**Spend Tiers:** Up to $50k/mo, $50k–$150k/mo, etc.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=1117.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Jason:**](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=473.0)
      - [Launch a LinkedIn ad experiment to validate the channel.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=473.0)
      - [Install the LinkedIn Insight Tag and configure conversion tracking.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=635.0)
      - [Define a tight experiment plan: one service, one goal, and a $5k–$10k/mo budget.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=473.0)
      - [Consider using dynamic HubSpot lists for retargeting open opportunities.](https://fathom.video/share/bbocyry7qyzCnzRPCuffF5mTnsWyF8bV?tab=summary&timestamp=1010.0)

action_items.description: Install LinkedIn Insight Tag; set conversions; upload company list; create HubSpot dynamic list; launch 1-2 LinkedIn lead-form campaigns; report to Ali
